title: Sending phone numbers via zapier to monday
excerpt:
Hey, I´m trying to send contacts with zapier to Monday but get many times problems with the phone number. 
Invalid value for {“phone”=>“+491765579XXXX‬”} on PhoneColumn. invalid value, please check our API documentation …

posts.author: ovokevinxo (Kevin Grosskopf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, I´m trying to send contacts with zapier to Monday but get many times problems with the phone number.

Invalid value for {“phone”=>“+491765579XXXX‬”} on PhoneColumn. invalid value, please check our API documentation for the correct data structure for this column. Guide to Changing Column Values

I tried the number with 0, +49 or nothing and its not working with this contact. Other accounts are working. I can´t find the problem.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @ovokevinxo,

I just created a Zap with a test account.

You can pass values like:

  • 4917655791111 DE
  • 12025550169 US
  • 541149030303 AR

And it works well.

image863×120 7.51 KB

Let me know if this helps!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: ovokevinxo (Kevin Grosskopf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not working

Invalid value for {“phone”=>“4917655791111‬”, “countryShortName”=>“DE”} on PhoneColumn. invalid value, please check our API documentation for the correct data structure for this column. https://developer.monday.com/api-reference/docs/change-column…

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again,

Can you please provide a screen recording or screenshots of your entire zap’s configuration (do not include any sensitive information).

Please mention me with a @ when you reply so I see the notification!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again,

I just tested this again using 17655791111 (as in what you sent) and it worked fine as well.

I will need you to please send screenshots or a screen recording to check your entire configuration since I can not reproduce the issue. I might be able to see what is causing the issue if I see this.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: ovokevinxo (Kevin Grosskopf)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Matias.Monday

With all the private data of the zap its a bit difficult. Can I send a screenrecord to you private somehow?

posts.cookedHtml:

Sometimes it is working and sometimes always the phone error. I used with both the same way to write the number down.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @ovokevinxo

Thank you for that information!

What happens if you do not use the plus symbol and use the number + the country name like this:

image863×120 7.51 KB

Do you get an error in that case?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @ovokevinxo

What happens if you use 4917655791111 DE?

Does that work?

Can you send a screenshot of what you are sending to the phone column (you can use a fake number if you don’t want to show the one you are using, but it has to have the exact same format and number of characters).

posts.author: mromska (P0rtugaliscalling32)
posts.cookedHtml:
Matias.Monday:

12025550169 US

Hello Matias.Monday,

I too am having the same issue with getting an error code coming through into Monday. I have attached 2 screenshots. One is a Zapier test that comes through fine with number, and the name is formatted correctly. IN the second screenshot you will see that I went through our contact us form through squarespace into Monday, and the results provided no number and the name field was coming through with the headers. Any advice on how we can change this.

We have also tried changing our form on our website to just have text fields instead of number format. We copied 4917655791111 DE from this last comment as well. Each time it errors.

Screenshot (270)1742×981 91.5 KB

Screenshot (272)1860×1008 83.1 KB

Screenshot (275)1715×1050 142 KB

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @mromska,

It looks like the issue lies on passing values between parenthesis.

Instead of (917) 257-1935, you should pass 9172571935 and the two letters for the country afterwards.

I am not sure how you can format this in Zapier before you send it our way, but I believe there could be a tool for that. You can ask Zapier’s team about it.

Can you please show me a screenshot of the zap using “4917655791111 DE” and showing an error?

posts.author: salfarraj (sal)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am having the same exact issue and can’t seem to make it work. @Matias.Monday

Here is the error I am getting, would really appreciate your help!

Now it seems to work when I run a test and it sends the zap out. However it doesn’t work when I use the published version. Meaning when I enter in the information on “encircle” app it gives this error. I’ve tried with a 1 in the front, US at the end, US with a space, tried without 1. It’s a USA phone field we are entering. xxx-xxx-xxxx

I also wanted to point out, the main entry we are putting this data in has an automated field with (xxx)123-1234 Meaning we can’t just type in the # without the ( ). Could that be the issue its having?

121849×960 96.9 KB

1231792×970 52.6 KB
